description: Business-domain MOLT Need blocks. Need answers: 'What must be true first?' Each block describes a business prerequisite, enabling condition, or required state that must exist before the commercial Aim can be achieved.
usage: Select the Need block that describes what must already be in place for the business Aim to succeed. If the Need is absent, the Aim cannot be reached regardless of effort.

MARKET_CONDITIONS.description: What must be true about the market before a commercial strategy can succeed

blocks.condition: A viable population of buyers with the problem and budget must exist before a GTM motion is launched
examples: ICP segment must be large enough to support revenue targets
market must have budget allocated to the problem category
buyer must experience the pain the product solves

blocks.condition: External conditions must be sufficiently favorable for the offering to gain traction
examples: regulatory change must have created urgency before entering a compliance market
buyer maturity must be high enough to purchase without extensive education
economic conditions must not be actively contracting the target segment

blocks.condition: Direct and indirect alternatives must be mapped before GTM positioning and differentiation can be defined
examples: battle cards must be built before sales launch
competitive pricing must be researched before proposal is submitted
alternatives must be inventoried before product roadmap is set

blocks.condition: Who the buyer is, what they care about, and how they buy must be confirmed with research before marketing or sales begins
examples: discovery interviews with 10 ICPs must be complete before content is built
decision-making process must be mapped before sales motion is designed
key objections must be known before sales scripts are written

blocks.condition: The target buyer must have enough awareness of the problem category for a solution-focused message to land
examples: buyers must know they have a data governance problem before a governance tool is pitched
market must understand AI risk before an AI safety offering gains traction
CFOs must be tracking the cost category before a cost-reduction play is credible

PRODUCT_READINESS.description: What must be true about the product before GTM or commercial activities begin

blocks.condition: A working, deliverable product must exist before sales commits to customers
examples: MVP must be complete before sales begins outreach
feature must be shipped before it is included in a proposal
integration must be built before it is promised in a contract

blocks.condition: The product must reliably produce the outcome that is being sold before customer acquisition scales
examples: the ROI claim must be validated with design partners before broad launch
the time-saving claim must be measurable before it is used in marketing
the outcome must be replicable across customer types before scaling customer success

blocks.need: Product is stable enough to support customers
blocks.condition: The product must meet a minimum bar of reliability and performance before customers depend on it
examples: P1 bug rate must be below threshold before enterprise launch
uptime must meet committed SLA before the SLA is sold
data handling must pass security review before enterprise contracts are signed

blocks.condition: A repeatable process for getting customers live must be in place before revenue is closed at scale
examples: implementation playbook must be written before CS team is hired
onboarding milestone framework must exist before customers are committed to go-live dates
training materials must be ready before first customer is handed off from sales

blocks.condition: Clear, approved pricing must exist before sales closes deals or marketing runs campaigns
examples: price list must be approved before proposals are sent
packaging must be defined before marketing communicates plans
discount authority levels must be set before reps begin negotiating

SALES_PREREQUISITES.description: What must be true before a sales team can effectively sell

blocks.condition: Enough salespeople must be in role and productive before a revenue plan is achievable
examples: AEs must be 90 days into ramp before they carry full quota
SDR team must be staffed before outbound targets are set
SE team must be hired before technical evaluations can be run in parallel

blocks.condition: Reps must have the knowledge, tools, and materials to sell effectively before they engage prospects
examples: demo environment must be configured before first customer meeting
sales deck must be approved before reps use it
competitive objection guide must exist before reps face bake-offs

blocks.condition: A sufficient volume of qualified opportunities must exist before a revenue forecast is credible
examples: 3x pipeline coverage must be present before quarterly forecast is trusted
SDR-generated pipeline must exist before AEs can hit their number
enough late-stage deals must be present before end-of-quarter close is achievable

blocks.condition: Pipeline data in the CRM must reflect reality before it can be used for forecasting or coaching
examples: all active deals must be logged before a pipeline review
stage definitions must be agreed before stage-based forecasting is trusted
contact records must be current before outreach sequences are run

blocks.condition: The person with authority to approve the purchase must be involved before a deal can be closed
examples: economic buyer must be identified before a commercial conversation is started
champion must confirm executive sponsorship before a proposal is submitted
procurement must be engaged before legal and finance review begins

FINANCIAL_PREREQUISITES.description: What must be true about financial resources and approvals before spending or committing

blocks.condition: Financial authorization must be in place before committing spend or resources
examples: headcount budget must be approved before a role is opened
project budget must be signed off before vendors are engaged
marketing spend must be authorized before a campaign is launched

blocks.condition: Unit economics and return projections must be tested and credible before an investment is committed
examples: LTV/CAC model must be validated before scaling paid acquisition
gross margin assumptions must be confirmed before pricing is set
payback period must be acceptable before a large tool purchase is approved

blocks.condition: Sufficient liquidity must exist to cover the commitment before it is made
examples: payroll for new hires must be fundable before offers are extended
vendor payments must be serviceable before a contract is signed
marketing budget must be available before a campaign is booked

blocks.condition: Revenue must be booked under the correct accounting treatment before financials are reported
examples: ASC 606 milestones must be met before revenue is recognized
ratable recognition rules must be applied before board financials are presented
deferred revenue must be correctly scheduled before investor reporting

blocks.condition: Approval and oversight mechanisms must be operating before significant financial commitments are made
examples: expense policy must be enforced before company cards are issued
procurement approval workflow must be active before purchasing begins
dual sign-off must be required before large vendor contracts are executed

CUSTOMER_PREREQUISITES.description: What must be true about a customer or account before engagement can proceed

blocks.condition: The prospect must be verified as a genuine fit for the product before significant sales resource is invested
examples: company size and tech stack must be confirmed before a demo is booked
use case must be validated as in-scope before a POC is agreed
budget must be confirmed before multi-stakeholder sales process begins

blocks.condition: The customer must have a clear sense of why this investment makes sense before they will approve it
examples: ROI must be quantified with the customer before a proposal is submitted
success criteria must be agreed before a trial begins
pain must be acknowledged at the executive level before final approval

blocks.condition: All compliance, legal, and security requirements must be satisfied before a customer contract is signed
examples: security review must be passed before an enterprise contract is executed
MSA must be negotiated before an SOW is signed
data processing agreement must be in place before customer data is processed

blocks.condition: A person inside the buyer organization who advocates for the purchase must exist before the deal can advance
examples: champion must be identified and tested before a proposal is sent
champion must have access to the economic buyer before final approval is sought
champion must understand and be able to articulate the value before being relied on internally

blocks.condition: The CS team must have bandwidth to onboard and serve a new customer before the deal is closed
examples: a CS manager must be available before a committed go-live date is given
implementation slots must be confirmed before contracts are signed
customer support capacity must be available before volume is committed

HIRING_TALENT.description: What must be true about people and roles before a hire can be made or a team can perform

blocks.condition: The job description, responsibilities, and success criteria must be documented before recruiting begins
examples: JD must be written and approved before posting
level and compensation band must be set before candidates are evaluated
hiring manager must agree on must-have vs. nice-to-have criteria before interviews begin

blocks.condition: Organizational and financial approval must be in place before a search is opened
examples: HC plan must be approved by finance before recruiter is assigned
backfill must be authorized before the role is posted
new role must be included in the board-approved operating plan before hiring begins

blocks.condition: A structured, consistent evaluation process must exist before candidates are assessed
examples: interview panel and scorecard must be defined before first candidate is screened
take-home exercise must be ready before the technical round
reference check process must be documented before offers are extended

blocks.condition: Market data on compensation must be researched before an offer is built
examples: salary range must be confirmed against market data before an offer letter is drafted
equity benchmarks must be reviewed before a senior hire is offered
total comp must be competitive before an offer is extended to a top candidate

blocks.condition: The manager who will onboard and support the new hire must have bandwidth before the hire is made
examples: hiring manager must not be overloaded before adding a direct report
manager must be able to provide a 30/60/90 plan before candidate accepts
team must have capacity to integrate a new member before close of search

LEGAL_CONTRACT.description: What must be true about legal and contractual conditions before a commitment is made

blocks.condition: A legally reviewed master agreement must be in place before deal terms are negotiated
examples: MSA must be approved by legal before sales uses it
standard SOW template must be ready before services deals are proposed
partner agreement must be finalized before partner program is launched

blocks.condition: In-house or external counsel must have reviewed the terms before signature
examples: non-standard terms must be reviewed before AE redlines are accepted
enterprise contract must be legal-reviewed before execution
acquisition agreement must be reviewed before signing

blocks.condition: All regulatory and policy obligations must be confirmed as satisfied before the contract is executed
examples: data protection requirements must be met before a DPA is signed
export control check must be complete before contract with international entity
required certifications must be in place before regulated industry contract is signed

blocks.condition: The right person must have authority to sign before a contract is executed
examples: signatory limit must be confirmed before routing for signature
board approval must be obtained before a major acquisition agreement is signed
dual-authorizer policy must be satisfied before a high-value vendor contract is executed

blocks.condition: Ownership of relevant intellectual property must be established before it can be licensed, transferred, or sold
examples: patent must be filed before IP is disclosed to a potential acquirer
work-for-hire agreements must be in place before contractor deliverables are used commercially
IP ownership must be confirmed before an investor due diligence process begins

OPERATIONAL_READINESS.description: What must be true about operations before a business unit or function can execute

blocks.condition: The steps to execute a business function must be written down before the function scales or changes hands
examples: billing process must be documented before it is transitioned to a new owner
onboarding playbook must exist before the first CS hire
procurement workflow must be documented before it is automated

blocks.condition: Required business tools and platforms must be set up before work can begin in them
examples: CRM must be configured before reps start logging activity
HRIS must be live before payroll is run
project management tool must be set up before a team is onboarded to it

blocks.condition: Clear decision-making and escalation routes must be established before complex or risky situations arise
examples: escalation tier for P1 customer incidents must be defined before go-live
discount approval chain must be set before sales launches
HR escalation path must be published before new managers are promoted

blocks.condition: What will be measured and how often must be agreed before a team or initiative is running
examples: success metrics must be defined before a new program launches
weekly reporting cadence must be established before a quarter begins
dashboards must be live before a team holds its first performance review

blocks.condition: How work passes between teams or stages must be clear before interdependent functions operate together
examples: sales-to-CS handoff must be documented before first customer is closed
marketing-to-sales lead handoff must be defined before campaigns launch
product-to-support handoff must exist before a new feature ships

STRATEGY_ALIGNMENT.description: What must be true about strategic clarity before execution begins

blocks.condition: Senior leaders must be aligned on direction before the organization executes against it
examples: C-suite must agree on the annual priorities before they are cascaded to teams
board must approve the three-year plan before headcount is built
exec team must agree on the ICP before GTM is designed

blocks.condition: Objectives and measurable key results must be defined and communicated before a team executes a plan
examples: company OKRs must be set before department OKRs are written
team OKRs must be agreed before individual goals are set
Q1 OKRs must be cascaded before the quarter begins

blocks.condition: The order of initiatives must be agreed before resources are allocated and work begins
examples: product priorities must be ranked before engineering capacity is assigned
market entry priorities must be sequenced before regional teams are built
investment priorities must be ordered before budget is allocated

blocks.condition: What will not be done must be explicitly accepted by decision-makers before a focused strategy is executed
examples: SMB deprioritization must be agreed before enterprise-only motion begins
international pause must be accepted before domestic focus is funded
product scope reduction must be acknowledged before an accelerated timeline is committed

blocks.condition: The relevant authority must explicitly acknowledge and accept a risk before a risky course of action proceeds
examples: exec must accept the downside scenario before a large bet is funded
legal must accept the regulatory risk before entering a gray area market
board must accept the burn rate before a growth investment is approved

PARTNERSHIP_PREREQUISITES.description: What must be true before a partnership can be productive

blocks.condition: Both parties must understand and agree on what each gains from the partnership before it is activated
examples: partner must see a clear financial incentive before signing
both parties must agree on the co-sell motion before joint customers are approached
integration value must be demonstrable to both sides before co-marketing begins

blocks.condition: A formal agreement governing the relationship must be signed before commercial activity starts
examples: reseller agreement must be signed before partner can sell
technology partner agreement must be executed before co-marketing is published
referral agreement must be in place before commissions are owed

blocks.condition: The partner must have the knowledge and tools to sell or deliver effectively before they engage customers
examples: partner must complete certification before running demos
reseller must have access to pricing and proposals before selling
SI must be trained on the product before running an implementation

blocks.condition: A technical connection between partners must be functional before joint customers are promised it
examples: API integration must be live before co-sell motion promotes it
marketplace listing must be tested before it is published
connector must pass QA before it is documented as supported

blocks.condition: A senior sponsor on both sides must be committed before a strategic partnership is launched
examples: VP-level sponsor must be identified before a strategic alliance is signed
exec alignment must be confirmed before joint pipeline targets are set
CEO-level sponsorship must exist before a co-development agreement is signed

INVESTOR_READINESS.description: What must be true about the business before engaging investors

blocks.condition: The company must be able to articulate a compelling reason to invest before investor conversations begin
examples: growth story must be coherent before a roadshow
market size and differentiation must be clear before an investor pitch
path to profitability must be articulable before a late-stage raise

blocks.condition: Accurate, up-to-date financial statements must exist before investor due diligence can begin
examples: audited financials must be available before a Series B
monthly board reports must be current before an M&A data room is opened
revenue recognition must be correct before financial due diligence

blocks.condition: Share ownership must be accurately recorded and free of complications before fundraising
examples: option plan must be fully documented before a financing round
any informal agreements must be formalized before investor review
pro-rata rights must be mapped before a new round term sheet is circulated

blocks.condition: Key performance indicators must demonstrate traction and trajectory before investors will commit
examples: ARR growth rate must show acceleration before a Series B is credible
NRR must show expansion potential before investors are excited about retention
CAC/LTV must be on a positive trend before growth investment is justified

blocks.condition: Customers who will speak positively about the product must exist before investor due diligence calls
examples: referenceable enterprise accounts must be secured before investor customer calls
case studies must be published before a funding announcement
design partner references must be available before seed due diligence

TEAM_PERFORMANCE.description: What must be true about team dynamics and structure before high performance is possible

blocks.condition: Each team member must know exactly what they own before the team can execute without confusion
examples: RACI must be agreed before a cross-functional project begins
territory and account assignments must be published before reps prospect
ownership of each OKR must be assigned before the quarter starts

blocks.condition: A baseline of trust between manager and team must exist before direct feedback and performance coaching can be effective
examples: 1:1 cadence must be established before performance conversations happen
new manager must demonstrate intent before the team will surface problems
trust must be rebuilt before a performance improvement conversation lands well

blocks.condition: How the team communicates — cadence, channels, decision rights — must be agreed before coordination can happen efficiently
examples: communication channels must be defined before a remote team starts work
meeting cadence must be set before a new quarter begins
how decisions are made and communicated must be clear before a project kicks off

blocks.condition: Clear, agreed standards of performance must be defined before anyone is evaluated against them
examples: quota must be set and acknowledged before the quarter begins
OKRs must be agreed before a performance review references them
promotion criteria must be published before anyone is assessed against them

blocks.condition: A clear and trusted path for resolving disagreements must exist before conflict arises
examples: escalation path must be documented before a cross-functional team is formed
how prioritization conflicts are resolved must be clear before roadmap debates
HR must be a known resource before a team-level conflict emerges

MARKETING_PREREQUISITES.description: What must be true before marketing can generate effective demand

blocks.condition: The specific profile of the buyer to be reached must be documented before campaigns are designed
examples: ICP personas must be written before content is produced
target account list must be built before ABM campaigns launch
segment definition must be agreed before paid media is configured

blocks.condition: The core message the company wants to communicate must be approved before external campaigns run
examples: positioning statement must be finalized before a product launch
value proposition must be signed off before sales materials are written
differentiation narrative must be agreed before competitive messaging is used

blocks.condition: Marketing materials must be produced and approved before a campaign goes live
examples: ad creative must be ready before a paid campaign launches
case study must be published before it is used in sales outreach
demo video must be complete before it is embedded in a nurture sequence

blocks.condition: How marketing-generated leads are handled after capture must be set before demand generation begins
examples: SLA for lead follow-up must be agreed before campaign generates leads
routing rules in CRM must be configured before leads start flowing
SDR handoff process must exist before inbound campaigns are running

blocks.condition: How marketing contribution to revenue is measured must be defined before ROI can be evaluated
examples: multi-touch attribution model must be agreed before campaign ROI is reported
UTM parameters must be implemented before source tracking is reliable
marketing influence definition must be documented before pipeline attribution is calculated

DATA_QUALITY.description: What must be true about business data quality before it can be used for decisions

blocks.condition: Key business metrics must have agreed, documented definitions before they are used across teams
examples: ARR definition must be agreed before it is reported across finance and sales
churn definition must be standardized before retention targets are set
active user definition must be locked before product metrics are used for decisions

blocks.condition: The origin and accuracy of the data being used must be confirmed before it informs a decision
examples: CRM data must be audited before forecast is submitted
financial data must be reconciled before board reporting
survey data must be statistically valid before conclusions are drawn

blocks.condition: The same numbers must appear in all reports before cross-functional discussions can be productive
examples: finance and sales must use the same revenue figure before a QBR
product and CS must use the same retention metric before a customer health discussion
marketing and sales must agree on pipeline numbers before GTM planning

blocks.condition: Prior period data must be accessible before trend analysis or year-over-year comparison is possible
examples: 12 months of revenue history must be available before forecasting growth rate
prior year employee data must exist before attrition trends are analyzed
historical pipeline data must be clean before sales velocity is calculated

blocks.condition: Customer data must be handled in compliance with privacy law and consent requirements before it is used
examples: GDPR consent must be obtained before marketing emails are sent
data residency requirements must be met before customer data is stored
data retention policy must be implemented before customer records are processed at scale

CHANGE_READINESS.description: What must be true before an organizational change can be successfully executed

blocks.condition: People must understand why the change is happening before they can commit to it
examples: the case for a reorg must be articulated before the restructure is announced
the reasons for a tool migration must be clear before training begins
the strategic rationale for a pivot must be shared before teams redirect their work

blocks.condition: Senior leaders must be unified and actively supporting the change before it is cascaded
examples: exec team must agree on the reorg before it is announced
all VPs must support the new strategy before it is communicated to their teams
leadership must model the new behavior before employees are expected to adopt it

blocks.condition: Everyone who will be affected by the change must be mapped before the communication plan is built
examples: all teams affected by a reorg must be identified before communications are drafted
all users of a system being replaced must be listed before migration planning begins
all customers affected by a product change must be identified before outreach

blocks.condition: Help for those going through the change must be available before the change goes live
examples: HR must be ready to answer questions before a reorg announcement
help desk must be staffed before a system migration goes live
manager training must be complete before performance management changes are rolled out

blocks.condition: How the success of the change will be measured must be agreed before it begins
examples: adoption rate targets must be set before a tool migration
post-reorg performance metrics must be agreed before the restructure
change ROI criteria must be defined before a transformation program is approved

REGULATORY_COMPLIANCE.description: What must be true about compliance posture before regulated activities begin

blocks.condition: Any legally required permits, licenses, or registrations must be in place before regulated activity begins
examples: financial services license must be secured before offering regulated products
data broker registration must be complete before data resale begins
healthcare certification must be obtained before processing PHI

blocks.condition: Policies, processes, and technical controls must be operational before compliance is claimed or audited
examples: access control policies must be live before SOC 2 audit begins
data encryption must be implemented before claiming HIPAA compliance
consent management must be deployed before GDPR audit

blocks.condition: A current, accurate privacy policy must be live before customer data is collected
examples: privacy policy must be published before a website collects contact details
data processing terms must be available before a B2B product goes live
cookie consent policy must be implemented before tracking is activated

blocks.condition: Staff must have completed required training before they handle sensitive data or regulated processes
examples: GDPR training must be completed before staff access customer data
anti-bribery training must be done before employees work with third parties in high-risk markets
security awareness training must be complete before remote access is granted

blocks.condition: Systems for capturing and retaining evidence of compliance must be running before regulated activities begin
examples: transaction logging must be live before financial activity is processed
communication archiving must be active before regulated correspondence begins
access logs must be captured before sensitive data is accessible to staff

ACQUISITION_INTEGRATION.description: What must be true before an acquisition or integration can succeed

blocks.condition: A thorough investigation of the target must be finished before a definitive acquisition agreement is signed
examples: financial due diligence must clear before a binding offer is made
legal and IP review must be complete before deal closes
technical and product due diligence must be done before integration planning begins

blocks.condition: A clear plan for combining the businesses must exist before close so Day 1 is stable
examples: Day 1 communications plan must be ready before deal closes
system integration roadmap must be agreed before close
org structure for the combined entity must be decided before announcement

blocks.condition: The cultural compatibility of the two organizations must be understood before integration design is finalized
examples: culture gap analysis must be done before team integration planning
values alignment must be assessed before merged leadership team is announced
people risks must be identified before retention plans are built

blocks.condition: Plans to retain key people through the transition must be finalized before deal close
examples: key employee retention packages must be agreed before close
founders must have clear post-close roles before signing
critical technical staff must have retention agreements before product integration begins

blocks.condition: All required government or regulatory approvals must be in place before a transaction can close
examples: antitrust clearance must be obtained before deal close
foreign investment approval must be secured before international acquisition completes
sector-specific regulatory approval must be granted before close

CUSTOMER_HEALTH.description: What must be true about a customer relationship before it can be managed proactively

blocks.condition: A clear plan linking the customer's goals to product outcomes must exist before the relationship can be managed
examples: 90-day success plan must be written during onboarding
renewal success metrics must be agreed before the first QBR
expansion targets must be documented before an account is designated for growth

blocks.condition: Sufficient engagement and usage signals must be captured before account health can be accurately assessed
examples: product usage data must be flowing before health score is meaningful
support ticket data must be available before risk is assessed
QBR completion rate must be tracked before engagement is evaluated

blocks.condition: The full set of contacts and their roles at the customer must be documented before relationship risk can be managed
examples: champion must be identified before an account is considered stable
economic buyer contact must be held before renewal begins
all relevant stakeholders must be mapped before a QBR is planned

blocks.condition: A senior relationship at the customer must be established before strategic expansion is possible
examples: exec sponsor must be identified before a multi-year renewal is pursued
C-level relationship must exist before an enterprise expansion motion begins
executive alignment call must happen before a cross-sell conversation is appropriate

blocks.condition: Open customer concerns, bugs, or commitments must be addressed before the relationship can progress positively
examples: blocking product issue must be resolved before renewal conversation begins
outstanding support commitments must be cleared before a QBR
contractual obligations must be met before expansion is proposed

LAUNCH_READINESS.description: What must be true before a business initiative, product, or market can launch

blocks.condition: Sales, marketing, product, and CS must be coordinated on the launch plan before go-live
examples: all functions must be briefed before a product launch
launch messaging must be consistent across all channels before announcement
sales enablement must be complete before reps are expected to sell a new product

blocks.condition: All defined pre-launch requirements must be satisfied before the launch proceeds
examples: all P0 bugs must be resolved before GA
minimum customer count for a logo must be secured before an announcement
required certifications must be obtained before a regulated product launches

blocks.condition: The support and success teams must be prepared to handle customer questions before launch
examples: support team must be trained on new product before launch
common objections must be addressed in a CS FAQ before go-live
escalation paths must be communicated before new customer volume begins

blocks.condition: All external-facing materials for the launch must be approved before they are delivered to customers or prospects
examples: launch email must be approved before it is sent
press release must be cleared by legal before it is distributed
in-product launch announcement must be reviewed before it goes live

blocks.condition: A way to reverse or pause a launch must exist before going live, in case of unexpected issues
examples: feature flag must be in place before a product launch
revert plan must be documented before a pricing change goes live
communication plan for a pause must be ready before launch

UMG_BUSINESS_NEEDS.description: Prerequisites for deploying UMG in commercial and organizational contexts

blocks.condition: The constraints that define what an AI business agent is and is not permitted to do must be written before deployment
examples: discount authority rules must be in governance before a sales AI agent is deployed
escalation triggers must be defined before a support AI is activated
communication policy must be encoded before an outbound AI agent runs

blocks.condition: The full sleeve — stacks, blocks, NeoBlocks — for the business agent must be architected before compilation
examples: sales agent sleeve must be complete before it is compiled for deployment
customer success agent blocks must be authored before the Envoy is activated
BI agent sleeve must cover the required analytical scope before use

blocks.condition: Business stakeholders must sign off on the governance rules before an AI agent operates under them commercially
examples: legal and compliance must approve AI communication policy before deployment
sales leadership must approve discount authority limits in governance before agent launches
finance must review pricing constraint blocks before a commercial AI is activated

blocks.condition: The success metrics for the AI agent deployment must be agreed before it goes live
examples: target metrics for AI-assisted deals must be set before a sales agent is deployed
support resolution rate targets must be agreed before an AI support agent is activated
BI agent accuracy benchmarks must be defined before it produces board-level outputs

blocks.condition: The conditions under which the AI agent hands off to a human must be defined and tested before deployment
examples: escalation triggers from AI to human AE must be tested before a sales agent goes live
support escalation criteria must be defined before an AI agent handles customer tickets
compliance exception handling must be routed to human review before a regulated AI agent is deployed
